---
title: "limabean"
format: html
editor: visual
---

```{r}
library(SpATS)
library(reshape2)
library(ggplot2)
library(openxlsx)
library(asreml)
library(dplyr)
library(tibble)
library(ggplot2)
library(metan)
library(agricolae)
```

```{r}
lbindetj = read.xlsx("C://Users//joaop//Desktop//fava//lbjoint.xlsx")
lbindetj$Block    <- as.factor(lbindetj$Block)
lbindetj$Env    <- as.factor(lbindetj$Env)
lbindetj$Genotype <- as.factor(lbindetj$Genotype)
lbindetj$GEN<- as.factor(lbindetj$GEN)
str(lbindetj)
```

```{r}
Traitsj = c("NDF", "NDM", "PH", "PL", "PW", "PT",
            "SL", "SW", "ST", "PN", "GY", "OHSW")
```

```{r}
aovj = anova_joint(lbindetj,
                      env = Env,
                      gen = Genotype,
                      rep = Block,
                      resp = everything())
```

```{r}
aov1 = get_model_data(aovj, "details")
```

```{r}
model2 <- gamem_met(lbindetj,
                   env = Env,
                    gen = GEN,
                    rep = Block,
                   random = "all",
                   resp = everything())
```

```{r}
plot(model2, type = "re")
```

```{r}
genpar = get_model_data(model2, "genpar")
```

```{r}
lrt = get_model_data(model2, "lrt")
```

```{r}
ranef = get_model_data(model2, "ranef")
```

```{r}
predict = predict(model2)
```

```{r}
vcomp = get_model_data(model2, "vcomp")
h2= gmd(model2, "h2")
gcor = gmd(model2, "gcor")
pcor = gmd(model2, "pcor")
details = gmd(model2, "details")
```

```{r}
#basic biplot
gge = 
  gge(
  lbindetj,
  Env,
  GEN,
  resp = c(NDF, NDM, PH, PL, PW, 
             PN, GY, OHSW),
  centering = "environment",
  scaling = "none",
  svp = "environment",
  by = NULL,
  
)
ggeplot = plot(gge,
     col.env = "orange",
     col.gen = "purple",
     arrow.color = "purple",
     size.text.gen = 3,
     size.text.env = 5,
     plot_theme = theme_bw())
ggeplot
```

```{r}
traits <- c("NDF","NDM","PH","PL","PW","PN","GY","OHSW")

gge_list <- lapply(traits, function(tr){
  
  gge_obj <- gge(
    lbindetj,
    Env,
    GEN,
    resp = tr,
    centering = "environment",
    scaling = "none",
    svp = "environment"
  )
  
  gge_plot <- plot(gge_obj,
                   col.env = "orange",
                   col.gen = "purple",
                   arrow.color = "purple",
                   size.text.gen = 3,
                   size.text.env = 5,
                   repel = TRUE,
                   repulsion = 1,
                   col.line = "orange",
                   plot_theme = theme_bw()) +
    ggtitle(paste("GGE-Biplot for", tr))
  
  return(gge_plot)
})

names(gge_list) <- traits
gge_list$GY
```

```{r}
dir.create("GGEplots", showWarnings = FALSE)

for(tr in names(gge_list)){
  ggsave(
    filename = paste0("GGEplots/GGE_", tr, ".pdf"),
    plot = gge_list[[tr]],
    width = 10,
    height = 8,
    dpi = 600
  )
}
```

```{r}
#Biplot type 2: Mean performance vs. stability
gge2 = 
  gge(
  lbindetj,
  Env,
  GEN,
  resp = c(NDF, NDM, PH, PL, PW, 
             PN, GY, OHSW),
  svp = "genotype"
)
gge2plot = plot(gge2,
                type = 2,
      col.env = "orange",
     col.gen = "purple",
     arrow.color = "purple",
     size.text.gen = 3,
     size.text.env = 5,
     repel = TRUE,
     plot_theme = theme_bw())
gge2plot
gge2plot2 = plot(gge2,
                type = 2,
      col.env = "orange",
     col.gen = "purple",
     arrow.color = "purple",
     size.text.gen = 3,
     size.text.env = 5,
     repel = TRUE,
     plot_theme = theme_metan_minimal())
gge2plot2
```

```{r}
dir.create("Mean_performance_vs_stability", showWarnings = FALSE)

# Loop por trait
for(tr in traits){
  
  # Calcular GGE para o trait
  gge2_tr <- gge(
    lbindetj,
    Env,
    GEN,
    resp = tr,
    svp = "genotype"
  )
  
  # Plot type 2
  p <- plot(gge2_tr,
            type = 2,
            col.env = "orange",
            col.gen = "purple",
            size.text.gen = 3,
            size.text.env = 5,
            repel = TRUE,
            repulsion = 1,
            col.line = "orange",
            plot_theme = theme_bw())
  
  # Salvar o plot
  ggsave(
    filename = paste0("Mean_performance_vs_stability/", tr, "_MeanPerformanceStability.pdf"),
    plot = p,
    width = 10,
    height = 8,
    dpi = 600
  )}
```

```{r}
#Biplot type 3: Which-won-where
gge3 = 
  gge(
  lbindetj,
  Env,
  GEN,
  resp = c(NDF, NDM, PH, PL, PW, 
             PN, GY, OHSW),
  svp = "symmetrical"
)
gge3plot1 = plot(gge2,
                type = 3,
      col.env = "orange",
     col.gen = "purple",
     arrow.color = "purple",
     size.text.gen = 3,
     size.text.env = 5,
     size.shape.win = 5,
          large_label = 6,
     repel = TRUE,
     plot_theme = theme_metan_minimal())
gge3plot1
```

```{r}
dir.create("Which_won_where", showWarnings = FALSE)

# Loop por trait
for(tr in traits){
  
  # Calcular GGE para o trait
  gge3_tr <- gge(
    lbindetj,
    Env,
    GEN,
    resp = tr,
    svp = "symmetrical"
  )
  
  # Plot type 3 (Which-won-where)
  p <- plot(gge3_tr,
            type = 3,
            col.env = "orange",
            col.gen = "purple",
            arrow.color = "purple",
            size.text.gen = 2,
            size.text.env = 5,
            size.shape.win = 4,
            large_label = 6,
            repel = TRUE,
            size.text.win = 4.5,
            repulsion = 2,
            col.line = "orange",
            plot_theme = theme_metan_minimal())
    ggsave(
    filename = paste0("Which_won_where/", tr, "_WhichWonWhere.pdf"),
    plot = p,
    width = 10,
    height = 8,
    dpi = 600
  )}
```

```{r}
#Biplot type 4: Discriminativeness vs. representativeness
gge4 = 
  gge(
  lbindetj,
  Env,
  GEN,
  resp = c(NDF, NDM, PH, PL, PW, 
             PN, GY, OHSW),
  centering = "environment",
  scaling = "none",
  svp = "environment",
  by = NULL,
  
)
gge4plot = plot(gge,
                type = 4,
     col.env = "orange",
     col.gen = "purple",
     size.text.gen = 3,
     size.text.env = 5,
     plot_theme = theme_metan_minimal())
gge4plot
```

```{r}
dir.create("Discriminativeness_vs_Representativeness", showWarnings = FALSE)

for(tr in traits){
    gge4_tr <- gge(
    lbindetj,
    Env,
    GEN,
    resp = tr,
    centering = "environment",
    scaling = "none",
    svp = "environment"
  )
    p <- plot(gge4_tr,
            type = 4,
            col.env = "orange",
            col.gen = "purple",
            size.text.gen = 5,
            size.text.env = 5,
            repulsion = 2,
            col.line = "orange",
            size.line = 0.5,
            col.circle = "black",
            plot_theme = theme_metan_minimal())
    ggsave(
    filename = paste0("Discriminativeness_vs_Representativeness/", 
                      tr, "_Type4_DiscriminativenessRepresentativeness.pdf"),
    plot = p,
    width = 10,
    height = 8,
    dpi = 600
  )}
```

```{r}
#Biplot type 6: Ranking environments
gge6 = 
  gge(
  lbindetj,
  Env,
  GEN,
  resp = c(NDF, NDM, PH, PL, PW, 
             PN, GY, OHSW),
  centering = "environment",
  scaling = "none",
  svp = "environment",
  by = NULL,
  
)
gge6plot = plot(gge,
                type = 6,
     col.env = "orange",
     col.gen = "purple",
     size.text.gen = 3,
     size.text.env = 10,
     plot_theme = theme_metan_minimal())
gge6plot
```

```{r}
dir.create("Ranking_Environments_Type6", showWarnings = FALSE)

for(tr in traits){
  
  # Criar gge usando apenas 1 trait por vez
  gge6_tr <- gge(
    lbindetj,
    Env,
    GEN,
    resp = tr,
    centering = "environment",
    scaling = "none",
    svp = "environment"
  )
    p <- plot(gge6_tr,
            type = 6,
            col.env = "orange",
            col.gen = "purple",
            size.text.gen = 3,
            size.text.env = 10,
            plot_theme = theme_metan_minimal())
  
  ggsave(
    filename = paste0("Ranking_Environments_Type6/", 
                      tr, "_Type6_RankingEnvironments.pdf"),
    plot = p,
    width = 12,
    height = 10,
    dpi = 600
  )}
```

```{r}
#Biplot type 8: Ranking genotypes
gge8 = 
  gge(
  lbindetj,
  Env,
  GEN,
  resp = c(NDF, NDM, PH, PL, PW, 
             PN, GY, OHSW),
  centering = "environment",
  scaling = "none",
  svp = "environment",
  by = NULL,
  
)
gge8plot = plot(gge,
                type = 8,
     col.env = "orange",
     col.gen = "purple",
     size.text.gen = 5,
     size.text.env = 3,
     plot_theme = theme_metan_minimal())
gge8plot
```

```{r}
dir.create("Ranking_Genotypes_Type8", showWarnings = FALSE)

for(tr in traits){
  
  # Criar gge para 1 trait por vez
  gge8_tr <- gge(
    lbindetj,
    Env,
    GEN,
    resp = tr,
    centering = "environment",
    scaling = "none",
    svp = "environment"
  )
    p <- plot(gge8_tr,
            type = 8,
            col.env = "orange",
            col.gen = "purple",
            size.text.gen = 5,
            size.text.env = 3,
            plot_theme = theme_metan_minimal())
    ggsave(
    filename = paste0("Ranking_Genotypes_Type8/", 
                      tr, "_Type8_RankingGenotypes.pdf"),
    plot = p,
    width = 12,
    height = 10,
    dpi = 600
  )}
```

```{r}
#Biplot type 10: Relationship among environments
gge10 = 
  gge(
  lbindetj,
  Env,
  GEN,
  resp = c(NDF, NDM, PH, PL, PW, 
             PN, GY, OHSW),
  centering = "environment",
  scaling = "none",
  svp = "environment",
  by = NULL,
  
)
gge10plot = plot(gge,
                type = 10,
     col.env = "orange",
     col.gen = "purple",
     size.text.gen = 5,
     size.text.env = 10,
     plot_theme = theme_metan())
gge10plot
```

```{r}
dir.create("Relationship_Among_Environments_Type10", showWarnings = FALSE)

for(tr in traits){
    gge10_tr <- gge(
    lbindetj,
    Env,
    GEN,
    resp = tr,
    centering = "environment",
    scaling = "none",
    svp = "environment"
  )
    p <- plot(gge10_tr,
            type = 10,
            col.env = "orange",
            col.gen = "purple",
            size.text.gen = 5,
            size.text.env = 10,
            plot_theme = theme_metan())
  
  ggsave(
    filename = paste0("Relationship_Among_Environments_Type10/", 
                      tr, "_Type10_RelationshipAmongEnvironments.pdf"),
    plot = p,
    width = 12,
    height = 10,
    dpi = 600
  )}
```

```{r}
blup = plot_blup(model2,
               prob = 0.2,
               col.shape = c("purple", "orange"),
               invert = TRUE)
blup
ggsave("blups.pdf",blup, width = 12, height = 10, dpi = 600)
```

```{r}
traits_blup <- names(model2)
dir.create("BLUPs_All_Traits", showWarnings = FALSE)
for (tr in traits_blup) {
  
  blup_plot <- plot_blup(
    model2,
    trait = tr,
    prob = 0.2,
    col.shape = c("purple", "orange"),
    invert = TRUE
  )
  
  ggsave(
    filename = paste0("BLUPs_All_Traits/", tr, "_BLUP.pdf"),
    plot = blup_plot,
    width = 12,
    height = 10,
    dpi = 600
  )}
```

```{r}
library(openxlsx)

wb <- createWorkbook()

for (trait in names(lbjoint)) {
  
  # 1) ANOVA
  if (is.data.frame(lbjoint[[trait]]$anova)) {
    addWorksheet(wb, paste0(trait, "_anova"))
    writeData(wb, paste0(trait, "_anova"), lbjoint[[trait]]$anova)
  }
  
  # 2) BLUEs / médias ajustadas (augment)
  aug <- lbjoint[[trait]]$augment
  if (!is.null(aug) && is.data.frame(aug)) {
    addWorksheet(wb, paste0(trait, "_BLUES"))
    writeData(wb, paste0(trait, "_BLUES"), aug)
  }
  
  # 3) Details (estatísticas extras)
  det <- lbjoint[[trait]]$details
  if (!is.null(det)) {
    # Converter lista para data.frame seguro
    det_df <- tryCatch(as.data.frame(det), error = function(e) NULL)
    
    if (!is.null(det_df)) {
      addWorksheet(wb, paste0(trait, "_details"))
      writeData(wb, paste0(trait, "_details"), det_df)
    }
  }
}

saveWorkbook(
  wb,
  file = "C:/Users/joaop/Desktop/fava/lbjoint_outputs.xlsx",
  overwrite = TRUE
)
```

```{r}
library(openxlsx)

# Pasta de saída
out_dir <- "C:/Users/joaop/Desktop/fava/model2_outputs"

# Criar a pasta se não existir
if (!dir.exists(out_dir)) dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE)

# Função auxiliar para transformar listas em data.frame quando possível
safe_df <- function(x) {
  if (is.data.frame(x)) return(x)
  if (is.list(x)) {
    tryCatch(as.data.frame(x), error = function(e) NULL)
  } else return(NULL)
}

# Loop por trait
for (trait in names(model2)) {
  
  wb <- createWorkbook()
  
  # Recuperar todos os componentes
  contents <- model2[[trait]]
  
  for (component in names(contents)) {
    x <- contents[[component]]
    
    # Tenta converter para data.frame
    df <- safe_df(x)
    
    # Se conseguir, salva como aba
    if (!is.null(df)) {
      sheet_name <- component
      # Excel não aceita nomes >31 caracteres
      if (nchar(sheet_name) > 31) sheet_name <- substr(sheet_name, 1, 31)
      
      addWorksheet(wb, sheet_name)
      writeData(wb, sheet_name, df)
    }
  }
  
  # Nome do arquivo final
  file_name <- paste0(out_dir, "/", trait, "_model2.xlsx")
  
  saveWorkbook(wb, file = file_name, overwrite = TRUE)
}

cat("Arquivos salvos em:", out_dir)
```

```{r}
library(openxlsx)

# Cria workbook
wb <- createWorkbook()

# Lista com todos os objetos que você quer salvar
listas <- list(
  VCOMP   = vcomp,
  GENPAR  = genpar,
  LRT     = lrt,
  RANEF_ENV = ranef$ENV,
  RANEF_GEN = ranef$GEN,
  h2 = h2,
  CorrG = gcor,
  CorrP = pcor,
  Details = details, 
  BLUPS = predict
)

# Para cada objeto: cria aba e grava
for (nm in names(listas)) {
  addWorksheet(wb, nm)
  writeData(wb, nm, listas[[nm]])
}

# Salvar o arquivo
saveWorkbook(wb,
             file = "C:/Users/joaop/Downloads/outputs_joint.xlsx",
             overwrite = TRUE)
```

```{r}
geeffects = ge_effects(lbindetj, Env, GEN, resp = everything())
plot(geeffects)
```

```{r}
corr = corr_coef(lbindetj, NDF, NDM, PH, PN, GY, OHSW)
corrplot = plot(corr) +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
    colours = c("orange", "white", "purple"),
    limits = c(-1, 1)
  )
corrplot
```

```{r}
ggsave("Correlation.pdf",corrplot, width = 4, height = 6, dpi = 600)
```

```{r}
winners = ge_winners(lbindetj, Env, GEN, c(NDF, NDM, NDF, NDM, PH, PL, PW, 
             PN, GY, OHSW))
winners
```

```{r}
winners2 = ge_winners(lbindetj, Env, GEN, c(NDF, NDM, PH, PL, PW, 
             PN, GY, OHSW), type = "ranks")
winners2
```

```{r}
gemeans = ge_means(lbindetj, Env, GEN, resp = c(NDF, NDM, PH, PL, PW, 
             PN, GY, OHSW))
gemeans
```

```{r}
write.xlsx(winners,  "winners.xlsx",  overwrite = TRUE)
write.xlsx(winners2, "winners_ranks.xlsx", overwrite = TRUE)
```

```{r}
library(openxlsx)

# Criar workbook
wb_means <- createWorkbook()

# Loop para adicionar cada tabela da lista
for (nm in names(gemeans)) {
  addWorksheet(wb_means, nm)
  writeData(wb_means, nm, gemeans[[nm]]$ge_means_mat)
}

# Salvar em .xlsx
saveWorkbook(wb_means, "ge_means.xlsx", overwrite = TRUE)
```

```{r}
vars <- lbindetj %>%
  select(where(is.numeric)) %>%   # retirar colunas numéricas que NÃO são respostas
  colnames()

# Lista de ambientes
envs <- unique(lbindetj$Env)

# Lista final dos resultados
tukey_results <- list()

for(v in vars){
  cat("\n\n=== Rodando variável:", v, "===\n")
  
  tukey_results[[v]] <- map(envs, function(e){
    
    df_env <- lbindetj %>% filter(Env == e)

    # Modelo simple ANOVA por ambiente
    mod <- lm(df_env[[v]] ~ df_env$Genotype + df_env$Block)

    # Tukey para Genotype
    tk <- tukey_hsd(mod, which = "df_env$Genotype")

    list(
      Env = e,
      Variable = v,
      Tukey = tk
    )
  }) |> set_names(envs)
}
```

```{r}
tukey_results$GY$Piracicaba$Tukey
```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(agricolae)
library(purrr)

traits <- c(
  "NDF","NDM","PH","PL","PW","PT",
  "SL","SW","ST","PN","GY","OHSW")

run_anova_tukey <- function(data, t1) {
  
  cat("\n============================================\n")
  cat("ANALYZING TRAIT:", t1, "\n")
  cat("============================================\n")
  
  envs <- unique(data$Env)
  results <- list()
  
  for (e in envs) {
    
    cat("\n----- Environment:", e, "-----\n")
    
    # Filter environment
    df_env <- data %>% filter(Env == e)
    
    # ANOVA
    mod <- aov(df_env[[t1]] ~ Genotype, data = df_env)
    an <- summary(mod)
    print(an)
    
    # p-value
    pval <- an[[1]]$`Pr(>F)`[1]
    
    if (pval < 0.05) {
      cat("→ Significant Genotype effect (p < 0.05). Running Tukey...\n")
      
      tuk <- HSD.test(mod, "Genotype", group = TRUE)
      print(tuk$groups)
      
      results[[paste0(t1, "_", e)]] <- tuk$groups
      
    } else {
      cat("→ Not significant. Tukey not applied.\n")
      
      results[[paste0(t1, "_", e)]] <- NULL
    }
  }
  
  return(results)
}

all_results <- lapply(traits, function(v) run_anova_tukey(lbindetj, t1 = v))
names(all_results) <- traits
```

```{r}
library(tidyverse)

winners_long <- winners %>%
  pivot_longer(-ENV, names_to = "Trait", values_to = "Winner")

library(scales)

lvls <- c("L01","L02","L06","L07","L10","L11","L19","L21",
          "L25","L27","L28","L30","L31","L32","L40")

# criar gradiente forte laranja → roxo
paleta_contraste <- colorRampPalette(c(
  "#F19E6A",  # laranja forte#F19E6A
  "#EDFF7A",  # rosa intenso
  "#90CEF5",  # roxo vibrante
  "#D48AFF"   # roxo escuro saturado
))(length(lvls))

# associar cores a cada Lxx
cores_winner <- setNames(paleta_contraste, lvls)

cores_winner
)
winplot = ggplot(winners_long, aes(x = Trait, y = ENV, fill = Winner)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_text(aes(label = Winner), color = "black", size = 5) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = cores_winner) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 14) +
  labs(
    title = "Winners Genotype per Trait and Environment",
    x = "Trait",
    y = "Environment",
    fill = "Winner"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 0, hjust = 1)
  )

ggsave("winners.pdf",winplot, width = 12, height = 10, dpi = 600)
```

```{r}
winners_ranked <- winners2 %>%
  group_by(ENV) %>%
  mutate(Rank = row_number()) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -c(ENV, Rank),
    names_to = "Trait",
    values_to = "Genotype"
  )
```

```{r}
ggplot(winners_ranked, aes(Genotype, Rank, fill = Rank)) +
  geom_col() +
  scale_y_reverse() +  # 1 = topo
  scale_fill_viridis_c(direction = -1) +
  facet_wrap(~Trait, scales = "free_y") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 14) +
  labs(title = "Ranking of Genotypes per Trait",
       y = "Ranking (1 = Best)") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5))
```

```{r}
top5 <- winners_ranked %>%
  filter(Rank <= 5)

ggplot(top5, aes(Rank, Genotype, color = Trait)) +
  geom_point(size = 4) +
  facet_wrap(~Trait, scales = "free_y") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 14) +
  labs(title = "Top 5 Genotypes per Trait (Across Environments)")
```

```{r}
gtb1 = gtb(lbindetj, GEN, resp = c(NDF, NDM, PH, PL, PW, 
             PN, GY, OHSW))

gtb1plot = plot(gtb1,
     col.env = "orange",
            col.gen = "purple",
            size.text.gen = 5,
            size.text.env = 8, repel = T,
            plot_theme = theme_metan())
ggsave("gtb1.pdf",gtb1plot, width = 14, height = 10, dpi = 600)
```

```{r}
library(openxlsx)

# Cria o arquivo
wb <- createWorkbook()

for(trait in names(aovj)){
  
  addWorksheet(wb, trait)

  # 1. ANOVA ------------------------------------------------------
  anova_tab <- aovj[[trait]]$anova
  writeData(wb, trait, "ANOVA", startRow = 1, startCol = 1)
  writeData(wb, trait, anova_tab, startRow = 2, startCol = 1)

  # 2. Modelo -----------------------------------------------------
  modelo <- broom::tidy(aovj[[trait]]$model)
  writeData(wb, trait, "Modelo_tidy", startRow = nrow(anova_tab)+4, startCol = 1)
  writeData(wb, trait, modelo, startRow = nrow(anova_tab)+5, startCol = 1)

  # 3. Augment ----------------------------------------------------
  augment_tab <- aovj[[trait]]$augment
  writeData(wb, trait, "Augment", startRow = nrow(anova_tab)+nrow(modelo)+7, startCol = 1)
  writeData(wb, trait, augment_tab, startRow = nrow(anova_tab)+nrow(modelo)+8, startCol = 1)

  # 4. Details ----------------------------------------------------
  details_tab <- aovj[[trait]]$details
  writeData(wb, trait, "Details", startRow = nrow(anova_tab)+nrow(modelo)+nrow(augment_tab)+10, startCol = 1)
  writeData(wb, trait, details_tab, startRow = nrow(anova_tab)+nrow(modelo)+nrow(augment_tab)+11, startCol = 1)
}

saveWorkbook(wb, "anovajoin_limabean.xlsx", overwrite = TRUE)
```

```{r}
gcormatrix = 
  tribble(
  ~NDF, ~NDM, ~PH, ~PL, ~PW, ~PT, ~SL, ~SW, ~ST, ~PN, ~GY, ~OHSW,
1, 1.082196764, 1.904462219, 1.958756158, 1.096834954, 0.596522684, 0.688338702, 0.60201591, 1.137264161, 0.058125602, 0.35933372, 0.756427151,
1.082196764, 1, 2.524498053, 2.804113654, 1.527589089, 0.543545253, 1.231182122, 1.030067359, 1.405148452, -0.581025294, -0.330069478, 1.280340508,
1.904462219, 2.524498053, 1, 3.142862688, 1.940355284, 0.483469775, 1.315300086, 0.990733011, 1.404611973, -1.381942679, 0.038958782, 1.450927149,
1.958756158, 2.804113654, 3.142862688, 1, 1.097409072, -0.020662118, 0.623754804, 0.143362478, 1.142978985, 0.284952996, 0.806286349, 0.903133917,
1.096834954, 1.527589089, 1.940355284, 1.097409072, 1, 0.467198658, 0.750049904, 0.6140841, 0.791715644, -0.525736861, -0.097127923, 0.881041714,
0.596522684, 0.543545253, 0.483469775, -0.020662118, 0.467198658, 1, 0.067039291, -0.204637952, 0.788953254, 0.14144793, 0.264966433, 0.290687621,
0.688338702, 1.231182122, 1.315300086, 0.623754804, 0.750049904, 0.067039291, 1, 0.841445874, 0.499735644, -0.602852647, -0.353148743, 0.957311203,
0.60201591, 1.030067359, 0.990733011, 0.143362478, 0.6140841, -0.204637952, 0.841445874, 1, 0.20873433, -0.530360894, -0.317000629, 0.825958308,
1.137264161, 1.405148452, 1.404611973, 1.142978985, 0.791715644, 0.788953254, 0.499735644, 0.20873433, 1, -0.604700475, 0.052663355, 0.692031363,
0.058125602, -0.581025294, -1.381942679, 0.284952996, -0.525736861, 0.14144793, -0.602852647, -0.530360894, -0.604700475, 1, 1.044227977, -0.526791071,
0.35933372, -0.330069478, 0.038958782, 0.806286349, -0.097127923, 0.264966433, -0.353148743, -0.317000629, 0.052663355, 1.044227977, 1, -0.023799045,
0.756427151, 1.280340508, 1.450927149, 0.903133917, 0.881041714, 0.290687621, 0.957311203, 0.825958308, 0.692031363, -0.526791071, -0.023799045, 1
)
corrmatrix = as.matrix(gcormatrix)
rownames(corrmatrix) = colnames(corrmatrix)
library(tidyverse)

corrlong = as.data.frame(corrmatrix) %>%
  rownames_to_column("Var1") %>%
  pivot_longer(-Var1, names_to = "Var2", values_to = "value")

corrgplot2 = ggplot(corrlong, aes(x=Var1, y= Var2, fill = value)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") + 
  geom_text(aes(label = round(value,2)), size = 3) + 
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low = "orange",
    mid = "white",
    high = "purple",
    midpoint = 0
  ) + 
  coord_fixed() + 
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) + 
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 0, hjust = 1),
    panel.grid = element_blank()
  ) + 
  labs(
    title = "Genetic Correlation Matrix",
    x = "", y = "", fill = "Correlation"
  )
```
